support_notes: Blocked on the standard pinned Docker 25 runner. Docker accepts the flag but warns that per-device block I/O weight is unsupported and discards the setting, so HostConfig.BlkioWeightDevice is empty.
Future verification requires a runner with per-device block I/O weight support and a stable host block device path.
Blocked on the standard pinned Docker 25 runner. Docker refuses container creation with this flag because the kernel does not support Docker CPU real-time scheduling in this environment.
Future verification requires a runner with Linux real-time scheduler support enabled and Docker configured for CPU real-time runtime.
Blocked on the standard pinned Docker 25 runner. Docker refuses container creation with this flag because the kernel does not support Docker CPU real-time scheduling in this environment.
Future verification requires a runner with Linux real-time scheduler support enabled and Docker configured for CPU real-time runtime.
Supported on the standard pinned Docker 25 runner. The create/inspect probe stores HostConfig.DeviceCgroupRules and round-trips the same rule.
Blocked on the standard pinned Docker 25 runner. Docker accepts the flag but the runner reports no io.max read-bps support, so HostConfig.BlkioDeviceReadBps is empty.
Future verification requires a runner with block I/O throttle support and a stable host block device path.
Blocked on the standard pinned Docker 25 runner. Docker accepts the flag but the runner reports no io.max read-iops support, so HostConfig.BlkioDeviceReadIOps is empty.
Future verification requires a runner with block I/O throttle support and a stable host block device path.
Blocked on the standard pinned Docker 25 runner. Docker accepts the flag but the runner reports no io.max write-bps support, so HostConfig.BlkioDeviceWriteBps is empty.
Future verification requires a runner with block I/O throttle support and a stable host block device path.
Blocked on the standard pinned Docker 25 runner. Docker accepts the flag but the runner reports no io.max write-iops support, so HostConfig.BlkioDeviceWriteIOps is empty.
Future verification requires a runner with block I/O throttle support and a stable host block device path.

remaining_work: See support reason for the known limitation.
support_notes: Create/inspect round-trip support is covered for --gpus=all on the standard pinned Docker 25 runner.
Starting a GPU container is not covered on the pinned runner because it has no GPU runtime or GPU hardware.
Runtime execution requires a GPU runner with GPU hardware and the Docker GPU runtime, such as NVIDIA Container Toolkit.
Blocked on the standard pinned Docker 25 runner. Docker refuses container creation because --storage-opt size requires overlay over XFS with project quotas.
Future verification requires a storage-specific runner using a Docker storage configuration that supports per-container storage options.
